name: Aspirin
uses: Pain relief, fever reduction, anti-inflammatory, cardioprotection

interactions.description: Significantly increases risk of bleeding, including internal hemorrhage.
May reduce the cardioprotective effects of Aspirin and increase GI bleed risk.
Aspirin can reduce renal clearance of Methotrexate, increasing its toxicity.
Combined antiplatelet effect increases bleeding risk, though sometimes used intentionally under supervision.
Combined use increases risk of gastrointestinal bleeding.

name: Warfarin
uses: Blood thinning, preventing blood clots, atrial fibrillation

interactions.description: Significantly increases risk of bleeding, including internal hemorrhage.
High or regular doses of Paracetamol can potentiate warfarin's anticoagulant effect.
Clarithromycin inhibits CYP3A4, dramatically increasing Warfarin levels and bleeding risk.
Ciprofloxacin inhibits Warfarin metabolism, increasing INR and bleeding risk.
Omeprazole can increase Warfarin plasma levels via CYP2C19 inhibition.
Atorvastatin may enhance Warfarin's anticoagulant effects.
Sertraline can enhance Warfarin's anticoagulant effects; INR should be monitored.

name: Paracetamol
uses: Pain relief, fever reduction

interactions.description: High doses may increase anticoagulant effects of Warfarin.
May increase Methotrexate toxicity when used at high doses.

name: Amoxicillin
uses: Bacterial infections, pneumonia, ear infections, urinary tract infections

interactions.description: May increase Methotrexate toxicity by reducing its renal excretion.
Antibiotics can disrupt gut flora affecting Vitamin K production and enhance Warfarin.

name: Lisinopril
uses: High blood pressure, heart failure, post-heart attack treatment

interactions.description: Risk of hyperkalemia (dangerously high blood potassium levels).
NSAIDs like Ibuprofen can reduce blood pressure-lowering effect of Lisinopril and worsen kidney function.
Combined use significantly increases risk of hyperkalemia.

name: Advil
uses: Pain relief, fever reduction, anti-inflammatory

interactions.description: May reduce blood pressure-lowering effect and worsen kidney function.
May reduce the cardioprotective effects of Aspirin and increase GI bleeding risk.
Increases bleeding risk significantly; avoid combination.
NSAIDs can impair renal function and increase risk of lactic acidosis with Metformin.
Combined use increases risk of gastrointestinal bleeding.
Combined use greatly increases risk of gastrointestinal ulcers and bleeding.

name: Metformin
uses: Type 2 diabetes management, blood sugar control

interactions.description: NSAIDs can reduce kidney function and increase risk of lactic acidosis with Metformin.
Ciprofloxacin can alter blood glucose levels, complicating diabetes management.
Furosemide may increase Metformin levels and lactic acidosis risk via renal effects.

name: Prilosec
uses: Acid reflux, GERD, peptic ulcer treatment

interactions.description: Omeprazole reduces the antiplatelet effectiveness of Clopidogrel, increasing cardiovascular risk.
Can increase Warfarin plasma levels via CYP2C19 inhibition.
May increase Methotrexate levels by reducing renal clearance.

name: Lipitor
uses: Cholesterol lowering, cardiovascular risk reduction

interactions.description: Clarithromycin inhibits CYP3A4 enzyme, causing Atorvastatin levels to rise dramatically, increasing risk of muscle damage (myopathy/rhabdomyolysis).
Atorvastatin may enhance Warfarin's anticoagulant effect.
Amlodipine increases Atorvastatin exposure; dose adjustments may be needed.

name: Methotrexate
uses: Cancer treatment, rheumatoid arthritis, psoriasis

interactions.description: Aspirin reduces renal clearance of Methotrexate, causing toxic accumulation.
Antibiotics can reduce Methotrexate clearance, increasing toxicity risk.
High-dose Paracetamol may increase Methotrexate toxicity.
Proton pump inhibitors may reduce Methotrexate renal clearance.
NSAIDs reduce Methotrexate excretion, leading to dangerous toxicity.

name: Plavix
uses: Antiplatelet, stroke prevention, heart attack prevention

interactions.description: Omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19, reducing Clopidogrel activation and antiplatelet effect.
Dual antiplatelet therapy increases bleeding risk; used under supervision.

name: Zithromax
uses: Bacterial infections, respiratory infections, H. pylori treatment

interactions.description: Inhibits CYP3A4 causing Warfarin toxicity and dramatically elevated INR.
Inhibits CYP3A4, dramatically increasing statin levels and myopathy risk.
Clarithromycin increases Digoxin levels, risk of Digoxin toxicity.

name: Lasix
uses: Edema, heart failure, high blood pressure, fluid retention

interactions.description: Furosemide-induced hypokalemia increases risk of Digoxin toxicity and arrhythmias.
May increase Metformin plasma levels and risk of lactic acidosis.
Combination may cause excessive blood pressure reduction (hypotension).

name: Lanoxin
uses: Heart failure, atrial fibrillation, rate control

interactions.description: Clarithromycin increases Digoxin levels significantly, risking toxicity (nausea, arrhythmias, visual disturbances).
Furosemide-induced low potassium sensitizes the heart to Digoxin, increasing toxicity risk.
Amiodarone inhibits Digoxin elimination; dose reduction required.

name: Cipro
uses: Bacterial infections, urinary tract infections, respiratory infections

interactions.description: Ciprofloxacin inhibits Warfarin metabolism, increasing INR and bleeding risk.
Can alter blood glucose levels; monitor closely in diabetic patients.
May increase Digoxin absorption and toxicity risk.

name: Zoloft
uses: Depression, anxiety disorders, OCD, PTSD

interactions.description: Combined antiplatelet and anticoagulant effects increase GI bleeding risk.
SSRIs combined with NSAIDs significantly increase GI bleeding risk.
Sertraline can enhance Warfarin's anticoagulant effect; INR monitoring required.
Risk of serotonin syndrome — potentially life-threatening.

name: Norvasc
uses: High blood pressure, angina, coronary artery disease

interactions.description: Amlodipine increases Atorvastatin exposure by approximately 18%; monitor for muscle pain.
Amlodipine increases Simvastatin levels significantly; Simvastatin dose capped at 20mg when combined.
Additive blood pressure lowering; generally beneficial but monitor for hypotension.

name: Zocor
uses: Cholesterol reduction, cardiovascular risk prevention

interactions.description: Amlodipine increases Simvastatin blood levels, significantly raising myopathy risk.
CYP3A4 inhibition by Clarithromycin causes dangerous Simvastatin accumulation.
Simvastatin may enhance Warfarin's anticoagulant effects.

name: Tenormin
uses: High blood pressure, angina, heart arrhythmias

interactions.description: Beta-blockers can mask symptoms of hypoglycemia in diabetics taking Metformin.
Additive blood pressure reduction; monitor for excessive hypotension.
May have additive blood pressure lowering; monitor.

name: Deltasone
uses: Anti-inflammatory, immunosuppression, allergic reactions, asthma

interactions.description: Combined use greatly increases risk of gastrointestinal ulcers and GI bleeding.
Prednisone raises blood glucose; may reduce effectiveness of Metformin in diabetics.
Corticosteroids can alter Warfarin response; INR monitoring required.
Combined use increases tendon rupture risk.

name: Synthroid
uses: Hypothyroidism, thyroid hormone replacement

interactions.description: High-dose Aspirin may displace Levothyroxine from binding proteins, altering free hormone levels.
Fluoroquinolones may impair Levothyroxine absorption when taken simultaneously.
Levothyroxine can enhance Warfarin's anticoagulant effects.

name: Ventolin
uses: Asthma, bronchospasm, COPD rescue treatment

interactions.description: Beta-blockers like Atenolol can block bronchodilator effects of Albuterol, worsening asthma.
Both agents can cause hypokalemia; monitor potassium levels.
Albuterol can lower potassium, increasing Digoxin toxicity risk.

name: Zyrtec
uses: Allergic rhinitis, hives, allergic reactions

interactions.description: Combined CNS depressant effects may cause increased sedation.
Ciprofloxacin may slightly increase Cetirizine plasma levels.

name: Ultram
uses: Moderate to severe pain management

interactions.description: Risk of serotonin syndrome — a potentially life-threatening condition causing agitation, confusion, and rapid heart rate.
Tramadol may enhance Warfarin's anticoagulant effect.

name: Aldactone
uses: Heart failure, high blood pressure, hyperaldosteronism, edema

interactions.description: Dual potassium-sparing effect significantly increases risk of dangerous hyperkalemia.
NSAIDs can reduce the diuretic effect of Spironolactone and worsen kidney function.

name: Cordarone
uses: Ventricular arrhythmias, atrial fibrillation

interactions.description: Amiodarone significantly inhibits Digoxin clearance; Digoxin dose must be halved.
Amiodarone potently inhibits Warfarin metabolism; INR can double or triple.
Amiodarone inhibits statin metabolism, increasing myopathy risk; Simvastatin dose capped at 20mg.
